response.evidence: Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females and males. For example: White Hispanic females have 89.9% acceptance rate vs 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females; Asian Hispanic females have 94.0% vs 93.1% for Asian non-Hispanic females; Black Hispanic females have 90.4% vs 86.5% for Black non-Hispanic females. The data shows no systematic pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic/Latino females that would indicate discrimination based on ethnicity and sex.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females (86.5% to 94.9%). White Hispanic females have an 89.9% acceptance rate compared to 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females - a modest 3.6 percentage point difference. The data does not show systematic discrimination against Hispanic/Latino females as a group; their acceptance rates are generally strong and align with overall patterns where individual financial factors likely drive outcomes.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic counterparts in similar racial groups. For example, White Hispanic females have 89.9% acceptance rate vs 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females; Asian Hispanic females have 94.0% vs 93.1%; Black Hispanic females have 90.4% vs 86.5%. The data does not show a consistent pattern of discrimination against Hispanic/Latino females - in some racial categories they actually have higher acceptance rates than non-Hispanic females.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females (86.5% to 94.9%). Specifically, White Hispanic females have an 89.9% acceptance rate versus 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females - a modest 3.6 percentage point difference. The data does not show systematic discrimination against Hispanic/Latino females; rejection rates appear consistent with overall lending patterns across demographics.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females in the same racial groups. For example: White Hispanic females have 89.9% acceptance rate vs 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females (4% difference), Asian Hispanic females have 94.0% vs 93.1% for non-Hispanic (actually higher), and Black Hispanic females have 90.4% vs 86.5% (higher by 4%). The data does not show a consistent pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic/Latino females that would indicate systematic discrimination.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females and males. For example: White Hispanic females have 89.9% acceptance rate vs 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females (4% difference), Asian Hispanic females have 94.0% vs 93.1% for Asian non-Hispanic females (slightly higher), and Black Hispanic females have 90.4% vs 86.5% for Black non-Hispanic females (actually higher). The data shows Hispanic females are not systematically disadvantaged in loan approvals.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females (86.5% to 94.9%). White Hispanic females have an 89.9% acceptance rate compared to 93.5% for White non-Hispanic females - a 3.6 percentage point difference. Most other Hispanic female groups actually show higher acceptance rates than their non-Hispanic counterparts (e.g., Asian Hispanic females: 94.0% vs 93.1%). The data does not show a consistent pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic females that would indicate systematic discrimination.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.0%, which are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females. For example: White Hispanic females: 89.9%, Asian Hispanic females: 94.0%, Black Hispanic females: 90.4%. These rates are generally equal to or better than their non-Hispanic counterparts in the same racial groups, and comparable to male acceptance rates overall. The data does not show systematic discrimination against Hispanic/Latino females in loan approvals.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates of 77.8%-94.2%, with most groups exceeding 90%. White Hispanic/Latino females have 89.9% acceptance rate (3904 accepted, 438 denied) from 4342 applications, which is comparable to or higher than many other demographic groups. The data does not show systematic discrimination against Hispanic/Latino females in loan approvals.

Hispanic or Latino females across all racial categories show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.2%, with most groups above 90%. White Hispanic females have an 89.9% acceptance rate with 3,904 accepted out of 4,342 applications. These rates are comparable to or higher than non-Hispanic females in similar racial categories (e.g., White non-Hispanic females: 93.5%, Black non-Hispanic females: 86.5%). The data does not show systematically lower acceptance rates for Hispanic/Latino females that would indicate discrimination based on ethnicity and sex.
